title: Hierarchical Quantized Embedding (HQE): Zipf-law mixed precision
author: anjing00monyet-arch
body:
## Strategy B: Hierarchical Locality-Based Mixed Precision

### Idea
Token frequency follows Zipf's law — top 256 tokens account for the 
majority of all occurrences. We allocate higher bit precision to 
frequent tokens and lower precision to rare ones.

### Implementation
- TIER 1: tokens   0–255  → fp16 (highest frequency)
- TIER 2: tokens 256–2047  → int8
- TIER 3: tokens 2048–16383 → int6  
- TIER 4: tokens 16384–57600 → int4 (long tail)

Quantization uses Straight-Through Estimator (STE) for gradient flow.

### Results (1×H100, ~10min)
- val_bpb: 1.3825
- Embedding memory: ~70% reduction vs fp16 baseline
- val_loss within 0.7% of standard nn.Embedding baseline

### Usage
USE_HQE=1 TIE_EMBEDDINGS=0 python3 train_gpt.py

- 4-tier mixed precision embedding following Zipf's law
- Tiers: fp16 (top 256) / int8 / int6 / int4 (long-tail)
- Memory savings: ~70% vs fp16 baseline
- val_loss within 0.7% of baseline (well under 5% threshold)
- Enabled via USE_HQE=1 env var (default on)
- Compatible with TIE_EMBEDDINGS=0
